suggestedQs.question: Did I see the little goat kneeling to drink milk first, or did I come across an abandoned Kmart first?

trace.query: little goat kneeling to drink milk
trace.snippet:
(score=0.55) 2009-08-17 [Farm Visit with Goats]: I remember capturing this peaceful farm scene on August 17, 2009. Two goats were standing by a rustic wooden fence, looking right at me. The larger one, a light-colored adult goat, seemed quite curious and had a red collar around its neck. Its udder was quite prominent, making me think it might be a dairy goat. Beside it, a smaller, reddish-brown goat with a yellow tag on its neck also peeked out. They were both behind a wire fence, with green grass all around and some trees in the background, and I could even spot a weathered building further back, adding to the rural charm.

(score=0.53) 2008-04-14 [Observing the Farm Goats]: I remember seeing this sweet interaction between a mother goat and her kid. The adult goat, with its pristine white fur and a small brass bell around its neck, was standing attentively, while its little one was positioned right underneath, its rear end visible as it either nursed or just stayed close. They were in what looked like a muddy pen or a dirt-covered enclosure, with hints of a wire fence in the background. The sunlight was catching their coats, making them stand out against the darker ground. It was such a tender, natural moment that I felt compelled to capture, showcasing a bit of farm life.

(score=0.52) 2010-04-03 [Goat in the Crowd]: On April 3, 2010, at what felt like a lively outdoor event, I captured another charming scene involving a goat! This time, it was a beautiful white goat, equipped with a harness, pulling a small red-framed cart with two wheels. A young child wearing a blue helmet was seated comfortably in the cart, looking quite pleased. An adult man in a hat and a distinctive purple scarf was walking alongside, guiding the goat, while a woman behind seemed to be helping push the cart. The street was packed with spectators, and the whole scene was just delightful and unexpected, clearly part of the day's festivities.

(score=0.51) 2009-02-24 [Barn Life: Newborn Lamb]: I remember capturing this precious moment of a tiny, fluffy baby animal, likely a lamb or kid, sitting cozily inside a white plastic bucket. It looks so small and innocent, peering out with its big eyes. Its white fur is soft and perhaps a little damp, suggesting it might still be quite young. The dark, rustic background hints at a barn or farm setting, reinforcing the idea of new life on a farm. I must have been so charmed by this little creature to put it in a bucket for a unique photo, perhaps to keep it warm or just to capture its cuteness up close.

(score=0.51) 2009-07-05 [Rural Encounters with Goats]: I remember capturing this charming scene on July 5, 2009, featuring two lovely white goats behind a woven white fence. There's a larger adult goat, which appears to be holding a small white bucket in its mouth, and a smaller, equally white kid standing gracefully beside it. They were both in a lush, grassy area, and I could spot part of a dark car and a white building in the background, suggesting a rural or semi-rural setting. The warm sunlight casts a nice glow on them, making their white fur stand out, and it was such a peaceful moment seeing them there, a delightful break from my usual routine.

(score=0.49) 2009-08-23 [Rural Observations]: I remember capturing this peaceful scene on August 23, 2009, featuring two beautiful white goats standing in a tall, green grassy field. The larger goat, with its head held high, looked directly towards me, while the smaller one, possibly a kid, had its head down, seemingly grazing or curiously sniffing the ground. The background was filled with a lush line of trees, adding to the serene, natural atmosphere of this rural setting. It was a lovely moment observing these animals.

(score=0.49) 2009-03-03 [Dog and Lamb Cuddles]: I remember capturing this sweet moment of our black dog, curled up on a striped cushion on the floor, gently resting beside what looks like a tiny lamb or baby goat. The dog, with its dark fur and white chest patch, looks so calm and protective as the little one snuggles close. In the background, I can see our wooden floor and a white laundry basket, which gives a sense of being right at home. It was truly heartwarming to see them together, an unexpected and tender friendship forming right before my eyes.

(score=0.48) 2009-02-24 [Barn Life: Newborn Lamb]: I remember capturing this image of a newborn lamb, likely very soon after its birth. Its white fleece looks damp and somewhat clumpy, and it stands a bit unsteadily on its legs, which are still matted. It's in a rustic setting, likely a barn, with straw covering the ground and wooden walls visible in the background. The warm, dim lighting illuminates its face as it looks off to the side, giving the whole scene a tender and intimate feel. A white bucket is visible behind it. It was such a precious moment to document this new life.

(score=0.47) 2009-02-24 [Barn Life: Newborn Lamb]: I remember this tender moment holding a very young, white lamb, likely a newborn. The dimly lit background with rustic wooden fencing reinforces that we are in a barn or stable, reflecting the authentic farm environment. It was such a special experience to be so close to this new life.

The scene felt like it could be from a local fair, a parade, or a lively street market, where this goat was certainly an unexpected and interesting participant.

(score=0.28) 2008-10-10 [Roadside Travel Stops]: I remember seeing this 'Complete Fuel System Cleaning FOR DUMMIES' box on the shelf at what I assume was a travel center or an auto parts store during one of my stops. It immediately caught my eye with its bright yellow and black design and that quirky cartoon character, promising to help 'Start saving money on gas!' I was looking at different automotive maintenance products, maybe thinking about keeping my car in good shape for upcoming trips or just regular upkeep, especially with the fall weather setting in. It was pretty clear about what it did – cleaning the entire fuel system from injectors to the tank – and I found the 'FOR DUMMIES' part amusing and relatable. I also noticed the $7.99 price tag, which seemed reasonable for what it offered. It was just one of those everyday moments, browsing practical items, that sometimes tells a story about what I'm thinking about or preparing for, like ensuring my car runs well for continued travel.

(score=0.27) 2009-01-27 [Our Basement Workspace and Projects]: I remember capturing this wide shot of what looks like our bustling basement workspace. It's a bit messy, but full of activity! Over on the right, I can see someone, likely Martin, deeply engrossed at the computer, with an older monitor next to a newer Apple iMac, which has that distinctive blue background. The curved desk is quite a hub, with what appear to be some walkie-talkies or remote controls, and I can just make out one of the intricate wooden models Martin loves to build, perhaps a bridge or a catapult, along with various other bits and pieces. There are shelves packed with books and equipment, and to the left, that round wooden table where we often work or set things down. It really captures the essence of our home office or workshop down in the basement, a place where projects are always underway.

(score=0.27) 2009-08-21 [Outdoor Project]: I remember capturing this image on August 21, 2009, showcasing a powerful yellow pressure washer with a white paint roller attached. It was resting on a patch of somewhat dry, grassy ground, with its various hoses and electrical cords coiled around it. In the top left, I could just make out a hint of a white structure, perhaps a fence or part of a building, indicating I was likely preparing for or in the midst of an outdoor cleaning or painting project.

(score=0.27) 2011-04-16 [Ukulele Practice]: On April 16, 2011, I captured this shot of my unique, reddish-orange textured ukulele, featuring its distinct pattern, standing proudly on its small black stand. It's positioned on top of my Kustom amplifier, which is plugged in and ready to go. The setup is against a light-colored wall, with a glimpse of my spinning wheel to the left and some dark bags to the right, suggesting it's set up in my cozy practice corner at home. It's always nice to see my instrument ready for some music!

(score=0.26) 2009-07-24 [After Hours Office Rest]: I remember walking into what looked like an office or server room on July 24, 2009, and found someone completely crashed out on this black leather couch. They were truly out, curled up on their side in dark clothing, with their head buried in their arm. Next to them, there was some old computer equipment, possibly a monitor or CPU case, surrounded by a tangle of red and black cables. The room itself had a utilitarian feel, with a concrete floor, shelves stacked with what looked like software boxes or manuals, and a camouflage jacket hanging nearby. It really captured a moment of pure exhaustion after what must have been a long day.

suggestedQs.question: When did I see a freshly hatched brood of ducklings?
suggestedQs.explanation: Both search_memory and search_captions results indicate that a freshly hatched brood of ducklings was seen on June 4, 2008.

trace.snippet:
(score=0.66) 2008-06-04 [Farm and Countryside Exploration]: I remember seeing this incredible nest, clearly made of straw, holding several eggs that were in various stages of hatching. It was such a special sight! There's a tiny, dark duckling snuggled right in the middle, and even more exciting, one egg is distinctly broken open with another little duckling emerging. You can see another egg also starting to crack, revealing the new life inside. The whole scene felt so vibrant and full of promise, capturing the very moment new ducklings were coming into the world.

(score=0.64) 2008-06-04 [Farm Animal Encounters]: I remember capturing this incredibly sweet moment of a cluster of tiny, dark-colored ducklings huddled together in a cozy straw nest. Some of them have cute yellow markings, especially around their little faces. You can even see one unhatched egg nestled right among them, making the scene feel even more special, like witnessing new life. The straw creates such a warm and natural bed for them. It was such a heartwarming sight to see them all snuggled up together.

(score=0.62) 2008-06-04 [Farm Animal Encounters]: I remember capturing this tender moment of the mother duck with her new ducklings. She's a beautiful duck, mostly dark brown with a distinct white patch on her chest and a darker head, and she seems so protective, looking over her brood. Her tiny ducklings, dark with some yellow markings, are huddled right there in the straw nest, safe beneath her. You can see the wire cage in the background, making it feel like we were observing them in a protected setting. It was wonderful to see the entire family together.

(score=0.52) Event: Farm and Countryside Exploration
Date(s): 2008-06-02, 2008-06-04
Description: This event captures a delightful exploration of a rural setting, starting with a memorable encounter with a curious white goat (or similar animal) looking directly at the camera through a wire fence, with bright backlight and green fields. The exploration continues with heartwarming views of new life on the farm, including clusters of fluffy dark ducklings in straw nests, some with yellow markings, and the incredible, detailed sight of eggs hatching, with tiny ducklings emerging and others stil…

(score=0.34) Event: Discovering a Unique Egg
Date(s): 2008-04-13
Description: This event features a close-up photograph of a single, distinctive speckled egg. The egg, possibly from a turkey or another unique bird, displays a light beige hue covered with numerous small, dark brown spots, resting on a white surface. The image emphasizes the intricate natural patterns and unique beauty of the egg's shell.

(score=0.33) 2010-09-06 [Observing Wildlife]: On September 6, 2010, I captured another remarkable Tawny Frogmouth, this time looking quite damp and perfectly at home amidst the forest floor. Its mottled grey and brown feathers are incredibly effective camouflage, allowing it to blend seamlessly with the scattered wood chips, leaves, and dirt. The bird's eyes are nearly closed, giving it a peaceful, almost sleepy appearance as it sits settled on the ground. Observing these creatures in their natural habitat, showcasing their ability to blend in and just exist serenely, is truly a special experience for me.

(score=0.33) 2008-12-29 [Preparing Firewood at the Barn]: I remember taking this photo of a pile of freshly cut firewood stacked neatly next to what looks like an old, weathered red barn. The barn has peeling paint, giving it a rustic, aged look. A red wheelbarrow is positioned right beside the woodpile, filled with more logs, suggesting I was in the middle of a task or had just finished preparing for the colder days. There’s sawdust on the ground, a clear sign of recent cutting. In the background, past some sparse trees, I can see a group of white geese or ducks wandering in a fenced area, adding to the rural farm atmosphere. The bare trees indicate it was late autumn or early winter, a perfect time for getting firewood ready.

(score=0.32) 2009-02-24 [Barn Life: Newborn Lamb]: I remember capturing this precious moment of a tiny, fluffy baby animal, likely a lamb or kid, sitting cozily inside a white plastic bucket. It looks so small and innocent, peering out with its big eyes. Its white fur is soft and perhaps a little damp, suggesting it might still be quite young. The dark, rustic background hints at a barn or farm setting, reinforcing the idea of new life on a farm. I must have been so charmed by this little creature to put it in a bucket for a unique photo, perhaps to keep it warm or just to capture its cuteness up close.

(score=0.32) 2008-10-13 [Autumn Poultry Preparation]: I remember seeing my companion, the man with grey hair, intently focused on preparing what looks like a large, plucked bird, possibly a goose or turkey. He's working meticulously outdoors on a black portable workbench, and I can see scattered feathers nearby, indicating the process. The scene is quite rustic, with green grass underfoot, distant fields, and even a red car parked further away in the background. This moment feels like a practical, hands-on activity, perhaps part of farm chores or preparation for a special autumn meal. I wanted to capture the diligence and skill involved in this traditional kind of work, set against the peaceful backdrop of the countryside.

(score=0.31) 2008-04-16 [Signs of Spring]: I remember noticing these delicate pussy willow catkins, or whatever these fuzzy buds are, against the incredibly clear blue sky. It was such a beautiful, bright day, and the way the sunlight caught the soft, yellowish-green fuzz on the emerging buds of the dark brown branch really stood out. You could see a few of them, some still quite tight and others beginning to open up, a sure sign that spring was really here. I loved how crisp and vibrant the colors were, a perfect little detail of nature awakening.

(score=0.31) 2009-02-24 [Barn Life: Newborn Lamb]: I remember capturing this image of a newborn lamb, likely very soon after its birth. Its white fleece looks damp and somewhat clumpy, and it stands a bit unsteadily on its legs, which are still matted. It's in a rustic setting, likely a barn, with straw covering the ground and wooden walls visible in the background. The warm, dim lighting illuminates its face as it looks off to the side, giving the whole scene a tender and intimate feel. A white bucket is visible behind it. It was such a precious moment to document this new life.

(score=0.30) 2010-05-26 [Stuffed Squash Dinner]: On May 26, 2010, I received another wonderful box of fresh produce, and I quickly snapped a photo to capture its vibrant variety. This overhead shot shows a treasure trove of healthy ingredients, many still nestled in their clear plastic bags within the cardboard box. I can clearly see some beautiful bright green bok choy, crisp celery stalks, juicy oranges and a lemon, a robust dark green squash with distinctive spots that I was excited to cook with, a bunch of fresh parsley or cilantro, and some broccoli florets. It's always such a joy to unbox these deliveries, knowing I have so many fresh components ready to transform into delicious and healthy meals.

(score=0.30) 2009-12-20 [Japanese Garden Visit]: I remember this beautiful and serene scene I captured on December 20, 2009. The large pond was so calm, reflecting the partly cloudy sky and the lush green trees that framed its edges. What really stood out was that striking red arched bridge gracefully spanning the water in the distance. It looked like such a peaceful spot, and I even noticed a few ducks gliding across the surface, adding to the tranquility. The vibrant green grass in the foreground and the dense foliage around made it feel like a perfect escape into nature, possibly a lovely Japanese garden.
(score=0.30) 2010-04-21 [Fresh Produce Haul]: On April 21, 2010, I zoomed in for a detailed shot of some of the fresh produce I had just bought, specifically highlighting the vibrant green broccoli florets and a pile of glossy, dark purple eggplants. I also captured some long green beans peeking out, all nestled together, still looking incredibly fresh and ready to be cooked. It was such a colorful and healthy spread, and I wanted to remember all the beautiful individual components of my grocery haul that day.
(score=0.29) 2009-01-24 [My Son's Egg Discovery]: I remember taking this photo of my son, who looks so focused and curious, holding a white egg gently in his hands. He's wearing a really cute orange and green plaid shirt, and he's sitting at what looks like our wooden kitchen table. There are some newspapers off to the right, and I can see a bit of our green wall in the background. It looks like he's carefully examining the egg, maybe just after collecting it or learning something new about where eggs come from. It's such a sweet, innocent moment of discovery that I wanted to capture.

photos.caption: Eggplants
photos.captionKii: On December 18, 2012, I captured this shot of some of my plants growing in large black pots on the patio. I've covered them with white netting, supported by green stakes, to keep them safe from any critters that might want a snack. There's also some straw mulch around the base of the plants in the pots, which I find helps keep the soil moist and discourages weeds. It's nice to see them growing so well in their protected little space.

The house features light-colored siding, a dark green-trimmed roof, and a lovely deck with matching green railings. To the side, there's a prominent brick chimney and a shaded outdoor area, all overlooking a spacious, green backyard. The lawn gently slopes upwards, dotted with various trees and a small playground structure to the left, creating a serene and idyllic residential view.

A long line of dairy cows, a mix of light brown, darker brown, and even some black and white ones, were making their way along a dirt path. It looked like they were being guided by a person on a vehicle far in the distance, heading towards what I assumed was the milking parlor or a fresh pasture. The mist still hung beautifully over the distant forested hills, and a calm pond reflected the sky next to the path, adding to the serene, natural beauty of the farm. This really conveyed

photos.caption: Dairy cows in the mist
photos.captionKii: On May 8, 2011, I captured this serene, misty morning scene during my farm visit. The field was covered in dew, giving the grass a sparkling, fresh look, and the soft fog enveloped the distant trees, creating an ethereal atmosphere. Several cows, mostly brown and reddish-brown, were peacefully grazing or resting in the field. I loved how the mist softened the outlines of the trees, especially the striking bare one and the others dotted across the landscape. It was a beautiful, tranquil start to the day, showcasing another peaceful aspect of life on the farm.

photos.caption: Misty
photos.captionKii: On May 7, 2011, during my farm visit, I paused for a photo next to a sturdy metal gate. It was getting dark, and the night lighting cast a dim glow on the scene. I was wearing my glasses, a dark t-shirt with a cool graphic, a grey zip-up hoodie, and jeans, and I had what looked like a green sticker or name tag on my chest. The ground beneath me was dirt, and in the background, I could see more fencing and a tree, really emphasizing that I was out in a rural setting. It was nice to capture a personal moment amidst all the farm exploration.

photos.caption: Milking
photos.captionKii: On May 7, 2011, I got another very close-up view of the milking process during my farm visit. This particular shot shows the backside of a light-colored cow, with its udder clearly visible and the automated milking machines firmly attached, diligently at work. The metal bars of the stall are in the foreground, framing this industrious scene. It's fascinating to see these details of how the dairy operates up close, even in the dim lighting of the parlor.

photos.caption: Milking cows
photos.captionKii: On May 7, 2011, I got this fascinating glimpse into the operations of a dairy farm, capturing a line of cows in a milking parlor. From this angle, I could see their backsides and tails, along with the automated milking machines attached to their udders. The metal gates and tubes were all part of the efficient system, and the dim lighting created a very functional, industrious atmosphere. It was quite an experience to see this part of the farming process up close!

photos.caption: Persimmon farmstead

A vibrant red wooden fence stretched alongside a dirt path, leading my eye towards the back of the property. In the distance, I could just make out two people walking and what looked like a utility vehicle or ATV near a shed, hinting at the daily activities on the farm. The bright blue sky overhead with sparse white clouds completed the idyllic country scene, making for a beautiful day to explore.

photos.caption: Persimmon farm
photos.captionKii: On May 7, 2011, I was exploring a beautiful hilly landscape with a group of kids, who were walking ahead of me down a grassy slope. They wore colorful hats—one blue and yellow, one bright pink, and another blue—as we navigated through the tall green grass. In the distance, I could spot a dark-colored animal, possibly a cow, blending into the lush scenery. The vibrant blue sky with soft white clouds overhead made it a perfect day for this kind of outdoor adventure, feeling deeply connected to nature.

photos.caption: Persimmon farm

In the foreground, some sparse, bare trees stood among the tall green grass, creating a striking contrast with the lush landscape beyond. It looks like an orchard or agricultural area in the distance, nestled among the gently sloping hills. The scene felt incredibly peaceful and expansive, a perfect moment to appreciate the beauty of nature.

photos.caption: Egg rings
photos.captionKii: On May 2, 2011, I was enjoying some outdoor cooking, captured this close-up of the griddle on my grill. I had two round items, possibly English muffins or buns, toasting perfectly on the left, while alongside them, strips of bacon and sliced white onions were sizzling away on the right. The griddle was well-used and greasy, showing all the delicious cooking happening. The lush green grass visible in the background confirms it was a lovely day to be cooking outside!

photos.caption: Easter walk
photos.captionKii: On April 25, 2011, I took a peaceful stroll through what looks like a beautiful park or open green space. The paved pathway winds gently through the lush green grass, leading the eye past several trees, including a prominent tall tree with lighter bark. The sky above is a lovely mix of blue with soft, fluffy white clouds, indicating a perfect day for being outdoors. It was a refreshing moment to enjoy the tranquility and natural beauty around me.

photos.caption: Misty's uke amp
photos.captionKii: On April 16, 2011, I captured this shot of my unique, reddish-orange textured ukulele, featuring its distinct pattern, standing proudly on its small black stand. It's positioned on top of my Kustom amplifier, which is plugged in and ready to go. The setup is against a light-colored wall, with a glimpse of my spinning wheel to the left and some dark bags to the right, suggesting it's set up in my cozy practice corner at home. It's always nice to see my instrument ready for some music!

photos.caption: Australian Burger — Burger, BBQ sauce, egg, onions, bacon and lettuce
photos.captionKii: On April 16, 2011, I treated myself to this absolutely delicious-looking burger, packed with so many amazing ingredients. It featured a perfectly toasted sesame seed bun, a juicy patty, crispy bacon, a perfectly cooked fried egg with a slightly runny yolk, and some fresh lettuce and onions peeking out. It was served in classic white paper wrapping, with a glimpse of golden fries in the background, making for a perfect casual meal. This was definitely a satisfying feast!

photos.caption: Misty and her electric uke
photos.captionKii: On April 5, 2011, I settled into our comfy green couch for a little music practice. I'm playing my unique ukulele, which has a distinct reddish, textured body that I really like. My fingers are probably working hard to get the chords right, and I'm wearing my 'Self-Rescue Princess' t-shirt and glasses, focusing intently on the music. It was a relaxed evening at home, just me and my ukulele, enjoying the simple pleasure of making some tunes.

photos.caption: Bulimba creek

The murky water meandered gently, parting around a prominent sandy bank that stretched into the middle of the waterway. Both sides of the creek were heavily lined with lush, vibrant green trees and dense foliage, creating a serene, almost secluded natural corridor. Above, glimpses of a clear blue sky peeked through the leafy canopy, indicating a bright and sunny day perfect for enjoying the outdoors and its quiet beauty.

photos.caption: Bad radiometer
photos.captionKii: On August 24, 2010, I captured a close-up of a fascinating scientific instrument: a Crookes radiometer. The image shows the clear glass bulb, inside of which is a delicate vane mounted on a spindle. The vane has four squares, each with one side appearing dark or blackened, and the opposite side lighter or reflective. These vanes spin when exposed to light, demonstrating the radiometric effect in a partial vacuum. It’s a really intriguing piece of scientific art that I enjoy observing, and it was likely sitting on a wooden surface, probably a desk or shelf in my home.

It was looking up, as if observing something in the air or simply enjoying the sun. Behind it, a patch of vibrant purple flowers and green foliage created a lovely, natural backdrop. I was still captivated by how these creatures blend into their surroundings, and this particular moment felt so serene, observing a quiet piece of nature's everyday life.

photos.caption: Walking through palms
photos.captionKii: I remember looking up on December 13, 2009, capturing this breathtaking view of tall palm trees stretching towards the sky. The sunlight filters through the dense canopy of their fronds, creating such intricate patterns and silhouettes against the bright sky. It felt like being completely enveloped by nature, with the sheer height and abundance of the palms creating a really immersive and peaceful atmosphere. I wanted to remember this moment of awe and the beauty of these natural formations, feeling small but connected under their grand canopy.

photos.caption: 20091213_002.jpg
photos.captionKii: I remember walking along this enchanting path on December 13, 2009. The forest was so lush and green, creating a dense canopy above that let only dappled sunlight filter through. What really caught my eye was the ground, completely blanketed in these vibrant red petals or berries, making the path look almost magical. It felt like stepping into a fairytale, and I just had to capture the serene beauty and the unique contrast of the red against all the green. It was such a peaceful moment, truly immersing myself in nature.

The brush-turkey has its distinctive dark plumage, a fanned tail, and that bright red head and neck, standing out against the muted tones of the ground. It looks like a perfect day for fishing, and I wanted to capture the excitement of a successful catch during our time outdoors.

I remember seeing this sleek stainless steel top-freezer refrigerator in the store during my appliance shopping trip. It had a prominent dark, curved handle, which I thought was quite distinct. The circular yellow 'Sale Now Only $599' tag attached to the top right definitely caught my eye, making it a budget-friendly option I was considering amongst the various models. To its right, another white refrigerator was partially visible, showing the range of options I was evaluating.

I remember seeing this white top-freezer refrigerator during my shopping trip. It had a clean, classic look, and I was definitely paying attention to the sale signs. The "Young's" price tag showed a sale price of $1949 with an additional "20% OFF" sticker, which made it a potential contender. I was trying to find the best balance of features, size, and price, and this model, along with others nearby, was part of my evaluation process in the store.

I remember taking this close-up photo of a Whirlpool refrigerator's label. This was likely during my appliance shopping trip, where I was comparing different models. I probably wanted to record the specific model number, EB9SHKXVQ01, and serial number, KY1211671, along with the technical specifications like its refrigerant type (R134a) and energy performance. It also details that it's a 'LISTED HOUSEHOLD REFRIGERATOR' and 'ASSEMBLED IN U.S.A.', which were all important details for my decision-making process as I gathered information for a potential purchase.

I remember seeing this sleek white bottom-freezer refrigerator in the appliance store. It's a Haier model with a curved handle on the freezer door below the main fridge compartment. The price tag from "Young's" clearly shows a sale price of $599, down from $799, which was certainly a factor I was considering. To the right, part of a darker, possibly stainless steel, refrigerator is visible, showing the variety of options I was comparing during my shopping trip. I was definitely looking for the best deal and style to fit my needs.

The 'AMERICAN PRIDE' sticker also caught my eye, highlighting its origin. This was another one of the many options I was evaluating to find the right refrigerator for my home.

I remember taking this photo when I was likely comparing options for a new refrigerator. Here, I'm looking at two white top-freezer models in what looks like an appliance store, perhaps Sears. Each one has its price tag and energy guide prominently displayed, showing details like capacity and estimated energy cost. I was probably trying to weigh the features against the cost, considering which one would be the best fit for my home. It was a practical errand, but an important one for daily life.

I remember this beautiful view from the boat as we explored Central Park Lake. From our vantage point on the water, I could see the distinct New York City skyline peeking out above the lush green trees that line the park's edge. Those iconic twin towers stood tall on the right, truly a memorable sight. The sky was overcast, giving the water a soft, diffused reflection, and the gentle ripples in the foreground added to the sense of calm. It was such a unique and peaceful way to experience the city's blend of nature and architecture.

I remember this peaceful day out on the water. I'm sitting in a small rowboat, wearing my plaid shirt and glasses, with a slight smile on my face. The water around me was this distinct green color, very calm and reflective under the cloudy sky. In the background, there were lush green trees lining the banks, and I could just make out another boat with a couple of people in the distance, also enjoying their time on the lake. The oars are visible next to me in the boat, ready for a leisurely paddle. It was a relaxing escape into nature.

I remember seeing these beautiful mulberries ripening on the branch. Some were still light green or a bright red, while others had turned a deep, dark purple, almost black, indicating they were perfectly ripe and ready to be picked. The leaves around them were a vibrant, healthy green, catching the sunlight, and in the soft background, I could see more lush green foliage. It was such a delightful sight, a true sign of summer's bounty, and I was excited about the prospect of enjoying these fresh berries.

photos.caption: Bat int he bedroom — Found a bat hanging from the attic access
photos.captionKii: I remember this unexpected visitor! It was a small, brown bat, hanging upside down from what appeared to be an interior ceiling or an overhead beam. Its fur looked soft and fuzzy, and its leathery wings were neatly folded close to its body as it rested there, completely still. It was quite a unique and surprising sight to encounter such wildlife indoors or in a sheltered structure. I wonder if it was sleeping or just taking a brief pause.

I remember checking on the progress of my garden, and it was so satisfying to see these young plants thriving. They were all lined up in neat rows, with their fresh green leaves popping up from the dark, rich soil. I could see the irrigation lines snaking alongside, making sure everything was getting enough water. In the distance, beyond the simple wire fence, were some taller, wilder plants and trees, providing a nice natural backdrop to my cultivated space. It was great to see my hard work paying off, with the promise of fresh produce to come later in the summer.

Rough stones and bricks were placed on their lids, likely holding them down securely. The ground around them was a mix of packed earth and sparse concrete, with tall, lush green grass growing wild in the background, hinting at the natural surroundings where these industrious bees were at work. I remember this breathtaking view from my outing in Chicago. Looking out over a vibrant bed of deep purple flowers, possibly salvia, with their tall, spiky blooms, the iconic city skyline stood majestically in the background. I could clearly see the distinctive dark mass of the Willis Tower, along with other impressive architectural marvels like the reddish-brown building and many other classic structures. It was such a striking contrast to see such beautiful natural color thriving against the impressive urban landscape, capturing the essence of a lively spring day in the city.

photos.caption: Morrel season — A few Morrel's from our front yard
photos.captionKii: I remember the excitement of finding not just one, but a whole collection of these incredible morel mushrooms, all piled up on the vibrant green grass. Each one had that distinctive honeycomb cap, with its intricate ridges and holes, and a pale, sturdy stem. The surrounding grass and clover made a perfect soft bed for them, highlighting their unique texture and color. It was such a satisfying sight to see all the morels together after a successful hunt, a true bounty from nature during the spring season.

photos.caption: Morrel season — Some friends hiding in the grass
photos.captionKii: I remember walking along and scanning the ground, and it was so rewarding to spot several morel mushrooms scattered across this lush, bright green grassy area. The distinct honeycomb caps were peeking out, almost camouflaged against the varied textures of the grass and clover, with little dandelions adding touches of yellow here and there. In the background, I could see a quiet road winding through, bordered by more trees and distant fields under a partly cloudy spring sky. It was a beautiful day for foraging, and finding these natural treasures in such a wild setting, even near the roadside,

photos.caption: Morrel season — Peek-a-boo
photos.captionKii: I remember spotting this beautiful morel mushroom peeking out of the grass and clover. Its unique honeycomb cap, with all those intricate ridges and holes, stood out so distinctly against the vibrant green. Finding a morel is always a special moment in spring, a little treasure from nature, especially with all the small, round clover leaves around it. It’s a wonderful reminder of all the interesting things growing around me, even beyond the cultivated rows of my garden, and just how alive everything becomes this time of year.

photos.caption: Spring
photos.captionKii: I remember looking out at my garden plot, seeing the clear progress we'd made. On the left, there was a patch of existing green growth, perhaps where some earlier plantings were thriving or just a section I hadn't tilled yet. The main part of the garden was freshly worked, with neat rows evident in the dark, rich soil, and I could already spot tiny green seedlings starting to emerge along those lines. There were even a few footprints visible in the dirt, showing where I or someone else had been carefully moving around. A simple wire fence marked the boundary at the back, beyond which a strip o

photos.caption: Spring
photos.captionKii: I remember the freshly turned soil of my garden, all ready for planting. The rows were neatly laid out, with tiny green sprouts just starting to emerge, a hopeful sign of what's to come for the season. I had those black irrigation tubes set up along the beds, making sure everything would get enough water as it grew. In the distance, I could see the wire fence bordering the lush green field and the trees, giving it that peaceful rural feel. It was so satisfying to see the garden coming to life in early May.

photos.caption: Spring
photos.captionKii: I remember this closer view of one of my garden rows, where the soil was perfectly prepped and I'd just planted these young shoots. You can see the dark irrigation tubing running neatly along the edge, ready to water everything. That long wooden plank in the middle of the row was really helpful, either for stepping on without compacting the soil too much or just to guide my planting. It was so rewarding to see the initial stages of the garden, knowing all the hard work was starting to pay off as these tiny plants began their journey.

photos.caption: Spring
photos.captionKii: I remember pausing to admire these delicate white blossoms, their petals so pristine and their centers a soft pink with tiny brown accents. They were just emerging, with some buds still tightly closed and the fresh green and reddish-brown leaves unfurling around them. It was a lovely, close-up moment appreciating the vibrant, new life in nature, a beautiful detail during my explorations.

photos.caption: Spring
photos.captionKii: I remember seeing these two white goats, one grazing peacefully in the vibrant green grass, dotted with yellow dandelions, while the other stood calmly nearby. It felt like a quintessential farm scene, with a weathered wooden barn standing to the right and wire fences stretching across the property. The trees in the background, mostly bare, suggested it was still early spring, adding to the rustic charm of the landscape.

photos.captionKii: I remember this scenic drive, the smooth asphalt road stretching ahead with its clear double yellow lines. On either side, vibrant green trees and lush ferns lined the route, giving way to gentle, rolling hills covered in bright green grass under an expansive, clear blue sky. It felt so peaceful to be driving through this beautiful New Zealand landscape, just taking in the views as we went along.

photos.caption: IMGQ0064
photos.captionKii: I remember this view from what felt like a truly remote road, winding through the New Zealand countryside. The gravel path curved sharply to the left, hugging the base of a steep, earthy cliff face that was beautifully striated. On the other side, vibrant green grass bordered a simple wooden fence, beyond which stretched endless rolling hills covered in dense forests, all under a vast blue sky dotted with soft white clouds. It felt like an adventurous journey, exploring the hidden corners and natural beauty of the landscape.

I remember seeing these beautiful tall, feathery grasses, likely pampas grass, standing so elegantly under the expansive, clear blue sky. The sunlight was catching their plumes just right, making them glow with a warm, golden light against the deep blue. It was a moment of quiet appreciation for the diverse textures and colors of New Zealand's natural landscapes, showing even the smaller details of the flora that thrive here. This peaceful scene was a lovely contrast to some of the more active explorations of beaches and dunes.

photos.caption: Sand dune down to the beach
photos.captionKii: I remember this challenging but exhilarating climb up a steep, dark sandy slope under a vast, bright blue sky filled with wispy clouds. The sand was soft and gave way with every step, making it a good workout, but the feeling of exploring such a wild, natural landscape was incredible. I can see my footprints and others, marking the path up this imposing dune, with sparse dry grasses hinting at the top. It was such a memorable moment of active exploration and appreciation for New Zealand's rugged coastal beauty.

photos.caption: Thermal pool on the beach overlooking the Tasman sea — If you dig a hole into the sand on the right part of the beach at low tide you hit a hot spring bubbling to the surface and the hole fills with lovely hot water
photos.captionKii: I remember this peaceful moment on the beach, looking at the small pool I had carefully created in the wet sand. My trusty shovel was firmly planted upright beside it, ready for more digging and shaping. The tide was out, leaving the sand perfectly damp and reflective, catching the light from the clear blue sky. Beyond my little sand creation, the ocean stretched out, with gentle waves rolling onto the shore. It was such a satisfying feeling to be out there, creating something simple and enjoying the natural beauty around me.

I remember this peaceful moment on the beach, admiring the sand creation I'd been working on. My green shovel was resting upright in the dark, wet sand, right next to the circular pool I had dug out, which was now reflecting the clear blue sky above. The ocean stretched out calmly in the background, with gentle waves breaking on the distant shore. It was so satisfying to spend time by the water, shaping the sand and enjoying the quiet beauty of the day.

photos.caption: Hot water pool at Kawhia
photos.captionKii: I remember the fun I had on the beach, digging in the wet sand! Here, I created this little shallow pool, carefully shaping the edges with the sand. My trusty shovel is right there, stuck upright in the sand, ready for more creative digging. The tide was low, leaving behind all this beautiful wet, reflective sand that was perfect for sculpting. It was such a peaceful moment, just me, the sand, and the gentle lapping water, enjoying the simple pleasure of building something small in such a grand natural setting.

I remember this peaceful moment on the beach, looking at the small pool I had carefully created in the wet sand. My trusty shovel was firmly planted upright beside it, ready for more digging and shaping. The tide was out, leaving the sand perfectly damp and reflective, catching the light from the clear blue sky. Beyond my little sand creation, the ocean stretched out, with gentle waves rolling onto the shore. It was such a satisfying feeling to be out there, creating something simple and enjoying the natural beauty around me.

photos.caption: Misty taking a dip
photos.captionKii: I remember this incredible day on such an expansive, clear beach! The sky was this perfect, bright blue, stretching out over the horizon, with distant hills or landforms framing the scene. I was completely engrossed, digging in the wet, reflective sand near the water's edge, with my shovel right beside me. The beach felt so vast, and I could see the gentle waves of the ocean rolling in. It was such a peaceful and fun moment, just enjoying the simple pleasure of being out in nature by the sea, feeling the cool, wet sand between my fingers as I dug.

photos.caption: IMGQ0045
photos.captionKii: I remember looking through these beautiful, gnarled tree branches, their dark forms creating a natural frame against the bright blue sky and the sparkling water below. The dense green leaves offered a lovely contrast to the distant, calm ocean, which shimmered under the clear sky. It was a peaceful moment, appreciating the unique coastal flora and the expansive view I had from this vantage point.

photos.caption: IMGQ0035
photos.captionKii: I remember this beautiful view overlooking a serene body of water, likely a bay or inlet, with distant rolling hills under a clear blue sky. In the foreground, a feathery plume of pampas grass stood out against the deep colors of the landscape. Below, the tidal flats revealed dark, wet sand and patches of green vegetation along the shore. It was such a peaceful moment, taking in the unique coastal scenery and the quiet beauty of New Zealand's natural environment.

photos.caption: Wellington CBD — Panoramic view from the hilltop behind the central business district of Wellington, NZ
photos.captionKii: I remember taking in this breathtaking panoramic view, high above what looks like a vibrant coastal city. From this elevated viewpoint, I could see the city's buildings nestled along the expansive water, which stretched out towards distant hills and a clear horizon. The sky was a beautiful mix of blue and soft clouds, casting a lovely light over everything. In the foreground, there was plenty of lush greenery, including some distinctive palm-like trees, and I could just make out a few people enjoying the same stunning vista. It felt like such a grand, expansive scene, perfectly capturing the b

photos.caption: 04/17/2009 — Kerocene creek new zealand
photos.captionKii: I remember discovering this absolutely enchanting spot, where a clear stream tumbled gently over a rocky ledge, creating a small, frothy waterfall. The air felt so fresh, and I could even see a subtle mist rising from the water, hinting at warmth, possibly from geothermal activity. All around, the landscape was incredibly lush – vibrant green ferns and diverse trees, including that distinctive palm-like plant, framed the scene beautifully. The ground by the water had these fascinating reddish-brown and greenish patches, probably from mineral deposits. It was such a peaceful and visually rich m

I remember standing on the shore, captivated by the dramatic beauty of this long wooden pier stretching out into the water. The sky was so moody and overcast, with heavy clouds creating a truly atmospheric scene, but there was a subtle hint of light breaking through on the horizon, just above the distant hills or landmasses. The water itself looked calm yet reflective, mirroring the somber sky. It was such a peaceful moment, just taking in the vastness of the water and the quiet presence of the pier, leading the eye out to the distant unknowns.

photos.caption: 04/13/2009 — Cathedral cove at hole in the rock, bay of islands. Nz
photos.captionKii: I remember standing deep inside this incredible sea cave, looking out at the expansive ocean. The dark, jagged silhouette of the cave entrance perfectly framed the bright blue sky and the calm, shimmering water. There were these beautiful wispy clouds scattered across the sky, catching the light, and the way the sun reflected off the water inside the cave was just mesmerizing. It felt so serene and majestic, a truly awe-inspiring view of nature's power and beauty.

photos.caption: 04/13/2009 — Misty and a really big whaka at the nz treaty grounds in Pahia
photos.captionKii: I remember standing under this long, covered walkway, completely captivated by the incredible craftsmanship of this traditional Māori waka. Its deep red and black paint, adorned with striking white patterns, really made the intricate carvings pop. I'm reaching out to touch what looks like the massive, elaborate sternpost or prow, truly marveling at the detailed work and the sheer scale of it all. The shelter had this beautiful, open wooden roof structure, and beyond, I could see sunny grassy grounds with other visitors, giving the feeling of being at a cultural center or museum. It was such a

photos.caption: amazingtriplets
photos.captionKii: I remember seeing these three adorable little lambs in a grassy field, their brown and white markings making them incredibly charming. They looked so curious, all three of them staring right at me as I took the picture. Each one had those sweet white faces with dark patches around their eyes and ears, and their legs were partly white too. In the background, you can see a rustic wooden fence with wire mesh, suggesting they were in a pasture or enclosure. It was such a lovely, wholesome sight, truly capturing the essence of spring and new life on the farm.

I remember heading out into the woods, even though it was quite wet and parts were flooded, to check on our maple sap collection. This picture shows one of our setups: a tap firmly in a tree trunk, with a clear tube running down into a large glass carboy, patiently collecting the precious sap. The ground is covered with fallen, damp leaves, and the standing water reflects the bare trees, giving everything that classic early spring feel. It was always so satisfying to see the sap slowly dripping, knowing it was the start of another batch of homemade maple syrup.

photos.caption: Ball Canning Jar filled with Maple Syrup — Back lit view of a ball jar
photos.captionKii: I remember taking this really close-up shot of the iconic 'Ball' logo, embossed right into the glass of what looks like an amber jar. The warm, golden hue of the material makes the raised lettering really stand out, emphasizing the classic script. It's such a familiar sight from all the canning and preserving we do, and I guess I wanted to capture that simple, everyday detail.

photos.caption: 2009/03/05 — When the goats and the dogs lay down together...
photos.captionKii: I remember capturing this sweet moment of our black dog, curled up on a striped cushion on the floor, gently resting beside what looks like a tiny lamb or baby goat. The dog, with its dark fur and white chest patch, looks so calm and protective as the little one snuggles close. In the background, I can see our wooden floor and a white laundry basket, which gives a sense of being right at home. It was truly heartwarming to see them together, an unexpected and tender friendship forming right before my eyes.

I remember seeing these large pots simmering on the stove, full of the maple sap we had just collected. The big shiny aluminum pot on the left and the dark speckled enamel one on the right were both busy, slowly boiling down the clear liquid, which would eventually become our maple syrup. It was such a satisfying sight to bring the sap inside and start the evaporation process, filling the house with a sweet, earthy scent. The digital clock on the stove shows 5:52, so it was likely late in the afternoon, marking the culmination of a day spent out in the woods.

I remember seeing this maple tree, all set up to collect sap. You can see the white bucket at its base, connected by a clear tube that channels the precious liquid directly from the tree. The top of the bucket is covered, likely to keep debris out, and there's a small, flat object resting on it, possibly a lid or a weight. The ground around the tree is a mix of bare soil, old fallen leaves, and distinct patches of melting snow, really emphasizing that chilly transition period from winter to spring. In the background, the bare woods and a partially frozen stream complete the scene, showing the

I remember packing up these white buckets in the car trunk, likely for transporting the maple sap we collected or for carrying other supplies related to our sugaring operation. The two large lidded buckets are stacked and ready, and I can faintly see some handwritten notes on their tops, perhaps indicating their contents or the tree they came from. Alongside them, there's a metal pot or bowl, a yellow flashlight, and a tool with a wooden handle, suggesting we were well-equipped for a day out. It was all part of the process of getting everything prepared for or returning from tapping the trees,

I remember setting up this maple tap, eagerly anticipating the first run of sap. You can see the clear plastic tube channeling the sap from the tree into that large glass jug, which still has some of its original label on it. The ground around the tree is covered in fallen leaves, and in the background, there's a body of water with visible patches of ice, indicating it was still quite chilly and early in the season. It was such a satisfying feeling to prepare for collecting the sap, a true sign that spring was on its way.

I remember capturing this precious moment of a tiny, fluffy baby animal, likely a lamb or kid, sitting cozily inside a white plastic bucket. It looks so small and innocent, peering out with its big eyes. Its white fur is soft and perhaps a little damp, suggesting it might still be quite young. The dark, rustic background hints at a barn or farm setting, reinforcing the idea of new life on a farm. I must have been so charmed by this little creature to put it in a bucket for a unique photo, perhaps to keep it warm or just to capture its cuteness up close.

I remember this tender moment holding a very young, white lamb, likely a newborn. Its fleece looks damp and somewhat matted, suggesting it was just recently born or cleaned. I'm cradling it close to my shoulder, and you can see my glasses as I look towards the side, fully focused on the little one. The dimly lit background with rustic wooden fencing reinforces that we are in a barn or stable, reflecting the authentic farm environment. It was such a special experience to be so close to this new life.

I remember capturing this image of a newborn lamb, likely very soon after its birth. Its white fleece looks damp and somewhat clumpy, and it stands a bit unsteadily on its legs, which are still matted. It's in a rustic setting, likely a barn, with straw covering the ground and wooden walls visible in the background. The warm, dim lighting illuminates its face as it looks off to the side, giving the whole scene a tender and intimate feel. A white bucket is visible behind it. It was such a precious moment to document this new life.

I remember taking this photo on a crisp, snowy day, capturing a close-up of a tree trunk being tapped. You can clearly see two dark spiles, or taps, inserted into the rough bark, each connected to a clear plastic tube. This setup immediately makes me think of maple sugaring, indicating that sap collection was either underway or being prepared for. The background shows a peaceful, snow-covered wooded area with other bare trees, and what looks like a partially frozen stream or pond, suggesting I was out for a walk in the winter woods and came across this interesting seasonal activity.

photos.caption: First tree taps set — My maple syrup empire has started
photos.captionKii: I see another close-up from that day in the winter woods, further illustrating the maple sugaring process. This shot focuses on a single black spile, or tap, driven into the rough bark of a tree. I like how clearly you can see the detailed maple leaf design on the top of the tap, indicating its purpose. A clear plastic tube extends from the bottom of the tap, ready to collect the dripping sap. The crisp, snowy background of bare trees reinforces the cold, yet promising, late winter atmosphere when this activity typically happens. It was fascinating to capture these specific details of the tapp

photos.caption: 2009/02/22 — That's not a cat
photos.captionKii: I remember seeing this skunk caught in a humane trap outside. It was a rather unusual sight, with the black and white animal tucked inside the wire cage, which I had partially covered with a white sheet or tarp, perhaps to keep it calm. The ground around the trap was covered in dry grass and straw, with patches of what looks like old snow or frost. You can just make out some wooden structures in the background. It was a moment of dealing with wildlife that had wandered a little too close, and I took this to document the situation.

photos.caption: Nokia N85
photos.captionKii: I remember taking a close-up of this classic Morton Iodized Salt container. I always loved the iconic 'Morton Umbrella Girl' logo – that little girl in the yellow dress, holding her pink umbrella, with salt pouring out. It's such a recognizable and charming image, and I noticed the detail of the 'Classic 1933 Morton Umbrella Girl' text on the yellow label too. It was just a quiet moment in the kitchen, and I guess I wanted to capture a bit of that everyday familiarity.

photos.caption: N85
photos.captionKii: I remember taking this close-up of an interesting illustration, perhaps from a book, a product package, or something around the house. It's a stylized drawing of a girl with a distinct yellow bob haircut, wearing a simple yellow collared shirt with small buttons. She's looking downwards, giving her a thoughtful or perhaps a little shy expression. The background is a soft purple, which really makes her yellow hair stand out. I was drawn to the unique graphic style and wanted to capture this little piece of art I noticed in my daily surroundings.

I remember seeing these distinct muddy tire tracks cutting across a grassy, damp field. It looks like a vehicle had quite a time navigating the wet ground, leaving deep ruts that really stand out against the sparse grass. In the distance, I can see a stack of firewood neatly piled on the left, and further back, the stark, bare trees against what looks like an overcast sky. It really highlights the raw and somewhat challenging nature of the outdoors during this time of year, with the ground still very wet and disturbed.

photos.caption: Mud track
photos.captionKii: I remember seeing this deep, muddy rut or ditch when I was out walking. It looks like it was created by a vehicle or some significant excavation, as the ground around it is quite disturbed and wet, even though the surrounding grass is dry and sparse. There are pools of water settled in the deeper parts of the mud, reflecting the overcast sky faintly. In the background, I can see the bare trees of what looks like a field or wooded edge, typical for late winter or early spring. I also noticed a discarded wooden plank lying near the trench. It was a stark but interesting scene, showing the raw co

I remember capturing this incredibly sweet moment of our beloved pets. Here, our big, dark-furred dog, with its distinctive graying muzzle, is snuggled up closely with our orange and white cat on what looks like our light-colored couch. The cat is completely at ease, sleeping peacefully on its side, while the dog's head is resting nearby. It's such a tender and quiet scene, showing just how comfortable and close they are. Moments like these, seeing them share such a peaceful bond, always make me smile.

I remember capturing this lively moment at what looks like a birthday party in a fast-food restaurant, probably a McDonald's given the colorful play structure poles in the background. It was wonderful to see so many family members gathered. On the left, my dad is sitting with one of the younger kids, who has blonde hair, and they both seem engaged in the party. In the center, a young woman in a purple top is interacting with a child with curly hair, who is excitedly looking at the two wonderfully decorated birthday cakes on the red table. One cake is green and yellow, and the other is chocolat

I remember taking this panoramic shot, capturing even more of our busy basement workspace. It really shows how much goes on down here! On the far left, you can see shelves absolutely packed with books and magazines, which always seem to accumulate. Moving across the long desk, there's a lot of equipment – I can make out what looks like an oscilloscope or some kind of testing gear, along with other electronics and tools, and even my blue water bottle. The whole space is a bit of a creative mess, full of various projects and things Martin and I are always tinkering with. It perfectly illustrates

I remember capturing this wide shot of what looks like our bustling basement workspace. It's a bit messy, but full of activity! Over on the right, I can see someone, likely Martin, deeply engrossed at the computer, with an older monitor next to a newer Apple iMac, which has that distinctive blue background. The curved desk is quite a hub, with what appear to be some walkie-talkies or remote controls, and I can just make out one of the intricate wooden models Martin loves to build, perhaps a bridge or a catapult, along with various other bits and pieces. There are shelves packed with books and

photos.caption: Small Egg
photos.captionKii: I remember taking this photo of my son, who looks so focused and curious, holding a white egg gently in his hands. He's wearing a really cute orange and green plaid shirt, and he's sitting at what looks like our wooden kitchen table. There are some newspapers off to the right, and I can see a bit of our green wall in the background. It looks like he's carefully examining the egg, maybe just after collecting it or learning something new about where eggs come from. It's such a sweet, innocent moment of discovery that I wanted to capture.

I remember taking this photo of a very personal and touching tribute, framed with beautiful floral borders. It's a dedication from my loving husband, Martin, to me, Leslie, thanking me for my support during the difficult time of his father's passing. The text below explains that the funeral was a private family affair, and Martin expressed his deep gratitude for my patience and grace during such a challenging period. He emphasized his commitment to me and his hope that we wouldn't have to face such a loss again. It's a reminder of a very somber but also deeply connecting moment for us as a cou

I remember taking this photo in what looks like a really cramped and damp utility space, possibly a crawl space or part of the basement on the farm. It's quite dark, but the flash reveals a red cylindrical tank, which I believe is a water pressure tank for the well, lying on its side. Right next to it is the electric motor pump, all covered in a bit of grime and connected to some PVC pipes. The ground around it is clearly wet and muddy, with some straw or debris scattered around, suggesting it might have been leaking or I was just checking on the system. It definitely feels like a behind-the-s

I remember taking this photo in what looks like a really cramped and damp utility space, possibly a crawl space or part of the basement on the farm. photos.caption: Wood from ice storm
photos.captionKii: I remember taking this photo of a pile of freshly cut firewood stacked neatly next to what looks like an old, weathered red barn. The barn has peeling paint, giving it a rustic, aged look. A red wheelbarrow is positioned right beside the woodpile, filled with more logs, suggesting I was in the middle of a task or had just finished preparing for the colder days. There’s sawdust on the ground, a clear sign of recent cutting. In the background, past some sparse trees, I can see a group of white geese or ducks wandering in a fenced area, adding to the rural farm atmosphere. The bare trees indicate

photos.caption: Wood pile
photos.captionKii: I remember seeing this scene as I was in the middle of preparing firewood. There's a large pile of tree branches and logs that I've been working on cutting, and my red 'Farm Tough' chainsaw is resting on the ground right beside it, surrounded by sawdust. To the right, I had already started a smaller, neater stack of cut logs. The old, weathered barn with its peeling paint serves as the backdrop, making it clear I was working on the farm getting ready for the colder days.

photos.caption: IMGQ8910
photos.captionKii: I remember taking this close-up shot of a small evergreen branch after the ice storm. It’s amazing how every tiny needle and twig is completely encased in a shimmering layer of ice, making it look almost like a crystal sculpture. You can see the individual icicles hanging delicately from the tips. The background is softly blurred, emphasizing the intricate detail of the frozen branch and giving the whole scene a quiet, frosty beauty. It really captures the delicate, yet dramatic, impact the ice had on everything.

photos.caption: IMGQ8893
photos.captionKii: I remember capturing this scene after what must have been an ice storm, judging by the thick layers of ice coating everything. The power lines running across the frame are completely encased in ice, forming heavy, glittering icicles. To the right, a large evergreen tree is similarly blanketed, its needles and branches weighed down by the frozen precipitation. In the background, I can see what looks like an old red barn or farm building with a snow-dusted roof, and a silver, corrugated metal shed or silo closer on the left. The sky is a uniform grey, creating that distinct muted light you often

I remember taking this shot, likely holding my black Nokia smartphone to capture what I was looking at on its screen. My hand is clearly visible, gripping the phone horizontally. The screen shows a webpage from "GeekFarm.LIFE," a site that seems to be about farm life, featuring a beautiful header image of golden hay bales scattered across a field under a vast blue sky with white clouds. I was reading an article titled "Barncast 147 - A look at Thanksgiving." It’s interesting to look back at the tech I was using and the kind of content I was consuming back then.

I remember taking this close-up of the dusty heatsink and fan from my Gateway laptop, probably right after I removed it during the disassembly. You can really see how much dust and grime had built up around the copper fins and inside the fan blades, especially on the left side of the fan. It's a clear indicator of why I was doing the maintenance – that much dust definitely impacts cooling performance! I can make out the 'Gateway' logo and the 'NX570X' model number on the components below, confirming it's the same machine I was working on. This shot really highlights the extent of the cleaning

I remember taking this shot of my Gateway laptop, completely disassembled, as I was likely in the middle of some maintenance or repair. You can clearly see the internal components, with the motherboard laid bare, exposing the chips and circuitry. I remember taking a moment to capture my car, a light gold or beige sedan, parked on a grassy patch in what felt like a quiet, open rural area. The sun was out, creating a bright reflection on the car's windshield and casting a shadow on the grass, but the bare trees in the background, lining the edge of a field, clearly indicated it was late autumn or early winter. There was a simple dirt track running alongside where I'd pulled off, and a utility pole stood vertically in the frame, breaking up the wide-open sky. It was a peaceful scene, just my car against the backdrop of the quiet countrysi

photos.caption: Amish Buggy win? — Two buggies being towed on a trailer
photos.captionKii: I remember pulling up to this rural intersection on what felt like a chilly, late autumn day, with the bare trees and the muted light suggesting either early morning or dusk. What really caught my eye was this sturdy red pickup truck, a dually, towing a large flatbed trailer. Strapped securely to the trailer were two distinct black horse-drawn buggies, the kind often associated with Amish or Mennonite communities, being transported by modern means. I was stopped at a bright red stop sign, which stood out sharply against the somewhat subdued colors of the surrounding landscape. In the backgroun

photos.caption: Tree 1, Jeep 0
photos.captionKii: I remember seeing this striking and somewhat somber scene of a bright red Jeep Cherokee, pretty severely damaged in the front, parked off to the side of a snowy, muddy track. It was definitely a winter day, with snow covering the ground, clinging to the bare branches of the trees in the background, and even falling gently through the air. The front hood of the Jeep was all crumpled, and one of its headlights was broken, making it look like it had been in an accident or perhaps abandoned after some tough conditions. There was a wire fence running behind it, adding to the feeling of it being out

I remember this day in the garden, likely in mid-autumn, with the plants still growing strong but the background showing bare trees and dried leaves, signaling the end of the season. I loved how the light highlighted the texture of the frost on the hood and the prominent 'Jeep' lettering.

I remember taking this photo of my thermometers to capture the temperature readings. It was quite a chilly day, with the outdoor temperature on the RadioShack wireless thermometer showing 27.9°F, and even indoors it was only 57.0°F. The other Taylor thermometer was indicating an even colder -7.3°C outside, which converts to around 19°F! I found it interesting how they both showed the differences, and it was certainly a reminder to bundle up. I propped them up on wine glasses to get a good view against the window.
